states.meaning: a fully promoted same-origin OPFS generation exists
a File System Access save closed successfully

failure_contracts.required_result: survival.mirror.failed receipt, promoted=false, generation cleanup, and no checkpoint
cancelled with no false export receipt

failure_contracts.condition: forced corpus, place, profile, or receipt failure during restore
failure_contracts.required_result: restore the prior records, assets, ledger, places, and profile, then emit survival.ark.restore.failed and no success checkpoint

streaming_contract.file_system_access: header, manifest, and each asset are written sequentially to the chosen file without creating one complete Ark Blob
streaming_contract.fallback: browser-download and in-memory creation are bounded to 268435456 bytes and fail closed above that limit
streaming_contract.retention: download-issued remains not_proven; only a successfully closed File System Access save is external

restore_contract.staging: validate header, payload, asset ranges, and duplicate hashes before mutation
restore_contract.commit: apply corpus, places, profile, and success receipt as one rollback-governed procedure
restore_contract.rollback: on any mid-restore failure replace every touched store and profile key from the pre-restore snapshot before recording failure

mirror_contract.promotion: write assets into a new immutable generation, write manifest.pending.json, then promote manifest.json last
mirror_contract.failure: clean the unpromoted generation and pending manifest, report exact writtenAssets and bytes, and keep IndexedDB canonical

external_observation_limits: The browser does not expose whether a conventional download was retained after the download request; download-issued therefore remains not_proven.
Repository tests can deterministically inject quota and interruption failures, but device-specific eviction policy and available storage remain browser and operating-system facts.
